title: Workhorse W56 Extended Wheelbase Model Achieves FMVSS and HVIP Certification
text:
Workhorse W56 Extended Wheelbase Model Press Release

Date: October 15, 2024

Key Announcement:

Workhorse Group announced that its 208-inch extended wheelbase W56 electric step van has achieved FMVSS (Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards) certification and received HVIP (Hybrid and Zero-Emission Truck and Bus Voucher Incentive Project) certification.

Production Status: Full production is underway at the Union City, Indiana facility, with deliveries scheduled for Q4 2024.

Vehicle Specifications: Payload capacity: approximately 10,000 pounds. Range: up to 150 miles per charge. Features over 100 enhancements compared to the original 178-inch model.

Target Markets: The vehicle is designed for last-mile delivery applications, particularly in parcel delivery and laundry services sectors.

Company Statement: Ryan Gaul, President Commercial Vehicles, noted that Achieving FMVSS and receiving HVIP certification for our extended wheelbase W56 is a critical step in bringing this vehicle to market.

Company Focus: Workhorse specializes in battery-electric commercial vehicles for last-mile delivery, along with cloud-based telematics systems for fleet optimization.
